headline: Nussmeier's precision guides LSU past Oregon in defensive grind
body: LSU's Garrett Nussmeier delivered when it mattered most, completing 16 of 29 passes for 212 yards and three touchdowns to steer the Tigers past Oregon 27-24 in a game that hinged entirely on offensive efficiency. Oregon outgained LSU 355 to 250 total yards and saw quarterback Bo Thomas throw for 236 yards with three scores of his own, yet the Ducks' 20-of-42 passing performance and inability to convert opportunities proved fatal. The difference came down to decisiveness in the passing game: Nussmeier worked with less volume but surgical accuracy, while Thomas faced a LSU secondary that forced difficult decisions downfield.

Wide receiver Cake was the critical beneficiary of Nussmeier's touch, hauling in seven catches for 117 yards and two touchdowns. That production came against an Oregon defense that generated pressure through Mothudi's three sacks but couldn't sustain drives into the end zone when it mattered. Oregon's rushing attack found more ground than LSU's, with Hughes gaining 49 yards on 11 carries compared to McNeil's 29 on five, yet that advantage on the ground was offset entirely by the Ducks' inability to maintain rhythm in the passing game.

Neither team forced a turnover, making this a rare clean-hands contest where execution determined the winner. LSU held possession and made fewer total plays but extracted more points per opportunity. Oregon will rue the 20-of-42 completion rate and the three sacks that stalled promising field position, while LSU's defense, anchored by Wood's eight tackles, bent enough to let Oregon reach the end zone three times but never fully broke.
turningPoint: Nussmeier found Cake for a second touchdown in the second half, extending LSU's lead at a moment when Oregon's offense had been unable to sustain consecutive scoring drives.

unsungHero.name: Wood
unsungHero.why: Eight tackles in a defensive effort that lacked sacks or splash plays, but his gap integrity and run responsibility limited Oregon's ground game and forced Thomas into longer third-down situations that shortened the Ducks' field position.
